mainEntity.name: Which IQCDL track is right for me?

acceptedAnswer.text: Foundation (3 days, no coding) is for leaders, CISOs, IT managers and compliance. Practitioner (5 days, hands-on) is for developers, cryptographers and security architects implementing PQC. If you're driving strategy, start with Foundation. If you'll be implementing, you'll want both.
IQCDL is aligned with NIST FIPS 203/204/205, ISO/IEC 4879, IEEE P7131/P7132 and the EU Post-Quantum Cryptography Roadmap, and is part of the IAIDL group active in 94+ countries since 2014.

mainEntity.name: Does the price change by country?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — IQCDL uses purchasing-power-adjusted regional pricing. Africa is capped at $45 per token, GCC is floored at $150 per token, and other regions are calibrated by market. See the full pricing booklet at https://iqcdl.org/pricing.

mainEntity.name: Is there a free way to start?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. The free "Quantum Computing for Everyone" course and the 2-minute Readiness Assessment require no payment or sign-up — both are linked from the Programs page.
